what_it_does: Defines the module constitution for mcp_face, prescribing a FastMCP agent-facing server that exposes type-generic verbs, delegates to a shared runtime Suite, and forbids tool-per-node-type expansion.
observations: Dir is named mcp_face to avoid shadowing the installed mcp SDK package.
It is a prescriptive constitution with register prescriptive, module mcp_face, status living.
Exposes generic verbs for AI operation; no tool-per-node-type.
Calls/shared runtime.suite.Suite (one brain, two faces: agent face + canvas UI bridge).
ToolAnnotations (readonly/destructive/idempotent) live in contracts/tools.py and feed governance; wired to SDK hints for sessions.py and channels.py.
Consolidated tool modules export a closed OPS constant per CONTRACT-FORMAT §9.2; sessions.py is the first honest instance, channels.py exports OPS + ACTIONS.
Operator-only actions (apply/approve) are deliberately not reachable from this face.
References Company Map, runtime constitution, and contracts constitution.
standouts: The generic-verb constraint: adding a node type adds zero tools; only genuinely new operation kinds get a verb.
One brain, two faces architecture: same Suite used by AI face and canvas UI bridge.
Closed OPS export obligation for tool modules with explicit drift tests.
conventions: Follows Vault Conventions by not duplicating the live verb set (points to Company Map).
Uses constitution document type with frontmatter tags/relates-to/status.
Names directory to avoid shadowing installed package (mcp_face vs mcp).
Breaks typical tool design by refusing tool-per-type and centralizing annotations in the contracts layer.
concerns: Several older tools still have pending face wiring for ToolAnnotations, creating a partially complete migration.
Older modules predate the OPS export obligation; reality extractor will fail loud when it lands, indicating current inconsistency.
Heavy forward-references to unshown files (Company Map, runtime/contracts constitutions) may make this file hard to use standalone.
notes: This is the prescriptive design contract for the AI-facing MCP server: generic verbs only, shared runtime brain, and strict rules about when a new tool may be added.
questions: Which exact verbs currently constitute the generic verb set? (File defers to Company Map.)
What is the full list of tool modules and which ones are older vs session/channel?
What does the reality extractor check and when will it land?
How are ToolAnnotations actually wired through to SDK hints for sessions/channels?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: it states it prescribes the agent face constitution and does so.
intention_signals: It is trying to keep the AI-facing MCP surface small, stable, and generic by forcing all operations through a shared runtime Suite and treating tool modules as governed, machine-auditable contracts.
novelty: The explicit rule that adding a node type/model/source adds zero tools, and the closed OPS constant export as a contract-format obligation for tool modules.
contribution: A prescriptive boundary document that defines the mcp_face module's scope, naming rationale, generic-verb design rule, relationship to the shared runtime Suite, and governance obligations for tool annotations and OPS exports.
summary_for_embedding: mcp_face/AGENTS.md is a living prescriptive constitution that defines the agent-facing FastMCP server module, mandating type-generic verbs, shared runtime Suite usage, no tool-per-node-type growth, and governed ToolAnnotation/OPS-export contracts.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to constrain and document how the AI operates the system through a thin, generic, governed MCP face that shares the same runtime brain as the canvas UI.
